#====================================================================================================
# START - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================

# THIS SECTION CONTAINS CRITICAL TESTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR BOTH AGENTS
# BOTH MAIN_AGENT AND TESTING_AGENT MUST PRESERVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK

# Communication Protocol:
# If the `testing_agent` is available, main agent should delegate all testing tasks to it.
#
# You have access to a file called `test_result.md`. This file contains the complete testing state
# and history, and is the primary means of communication between main and the testing agent.
#
# Main and testing agents must follow this exact format to maintain testing data. 
# The testing data must be entered in yaml format Below is the data structure:
# 
## user_problem_statement: {problem_statement}
## backend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.py"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## frontend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.js"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## metadata:
##   created_by: "main_agent"
##   version: "1.0"
##   test_sequence: 0
##   run_ui: false
##
## test_plan:
##   current_focus:
##     - "Task name 1"
##     - "Task name 2"
##   stuck_tasks:
##     - "Task name with persistent issues"
##   test_all: false
##   test_priority: "high_first"  # or "sequential" or "stuck_first"
##
## agent_communication:
##     -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##     -message: "Communication message between agents"

# Protocol Guidelines for Main agent
#
# 1. Update Test Result File Before Testing:
#    - Main agent must always update the `test_result.md` file before calling the testing agent
#    - Add implementation details to the status_history
#    - Set `needs_retesting` to true for tasks that need testing
#    - Update the `test_plan` section to guide testing priorities
#    - Add a message to `agent_communication` explaining what you've done
#
# 2. Incorporate User Feedback:
#    - When a user provides feedback that something is or isn't working, add this information to the relevant task's status_history
#    - Update the working status based on user feedback
#    - If a user reports an issue with a task that was marked as working, increment the stuck_count
#    - Whenever user reports issue in the app, if we have testing agent and task_result.md file so find the appropriate task for that and append in status_history of that task to contain the user concern and problem as well 
#
# 3. Track Stuck Tasks:
#    - Monitor which tasks have high stuck_count values or where you are fixing same issue again and again, analyze that when you read task_result.md
#    - For persistent issues, use websearch tool to find solutions
#    - Pay special attention to tasks in the stuck_tasks list
#    - When you fix an issue with a stuck task, don't reset the stuck_count until the testing agent confirms it's working
#
# 4. Provide Context to Testing Agent:
#    - When calling the testing agent, provide clear instructions about:
#      - Which tasks need testing (reference the test_plan)
#      - Any authentication details or configuration needed
#      - Specific test scenarios to focus on
#      - Any known issues or edge cases to verify
#
# 5. Call the testing agent with specific instructions referring to test_result.md
#
# IMPORTANT: Main agent must ALWAYS update test_result.md BEFORE calling the testing agent, as it relies on this file to understand what to test next.

#====================================================================================================
# END - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================



#====================================================================================================
# Testing Data - Main Agent and testing sub agent both should log testing data below this section
#====================================================================================================

user_problem_statement: "Test the PMLL Memory Graph FastAPI backend at the internal URL http://localhost:8001 (all endpoints under /api prefix). This backend implements a persistent memory layer with short-term KV silo (in-memory per session), long-term memory graph (MongoDB), TF-IDF semantic search, typed graph edges with temporal decay."

backend:
  - task: "Root endpoint health check"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "GET /api/ returns correct service identification {service:'pmll-memory-graph', ok:true}"

  - task: "Session initialization"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/init successfully initializes session with silo_size parameter"

  - task: "Short-term memory KV operations"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/set and POST /api/peek work correctly for both existing and missing keys"

  - task: "Promise resolution system"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/resolve creates promises with pending status correctly"

  - task: "Memory node management"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/upsert_memory_node successfully creates concept nodes with proper UUID generation"

  - task: "Graph relation management"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/create_relation validates relations correctly, accepts valid relations (depends_on) and rejects invalid ones (foobar) with 400 status"

  - task: "TF-IDF semantic search"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/search_memory_graph returns semantic matches with direct and neighbor results using TF-IDF cosine similarity"

  - task: "Bulk context insertion with auto-linking"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/add_interlinked_context inserts multiple nodes and performs auto-linking based on similarity threshold (0.72)"

  - task: "Graph traversal system"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/retrieve_with_traversal performs BFS traversal with depth limits and returns reachable nodes"

  - task: "Context resolution (short-term vs long-term)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/resolve_context correctly identifies short-term vs long-term sources for context resolution"

  - task: "Memory promotion system"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/promote_to_long_term successfully promotes short-term entries to long-term memory nodes"

  - task: "Memory status reporting"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "low"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/memory_status returns comprehensive statistics for both short-term and long-term memory"

  - task: "Graph visualization endpoints"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "low"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "GET /api/graph/{session_id} and GET /api/silo/{session_id} return graph data with temporal decay weights"

  - task: "GraphQL query interface"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/graphql supports nodes and search queries with proper variable handling"

  - task: "Temporal decay and pruning"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/prune_stale_links implements temporal decay (e^(-lambda*t)) and removes stale edges and orphan nodes"

  - task: "Session management"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "medium"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/flush successfully clears session data (silo and promises)"

  - task: "Demo seeding system"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "low"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "POST /api/seed/{session_id} creates 12 demo nodes with 9+ explicit edges and supports semantic search"

frontend:
  # No frontend testing performed as per instructions

metadata:
  created_by: "testing_agent"
  version: "1.0"
  test_sequence: 1
  run_ui: false

test_plan:
  current_focus: []
  stuck_tasks: []
  test_all: true
  test_priority: "completed"

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Comprehensive backend testing completed successfully. All 24 API endpoints tested and working correctly. The PMLL Memory Graph FastAPI backend is fully functional with proper MongoDB integration, TF-IDF semantic search, temporal decay, and all specified features working as expected. Backend URL: https://semantic-silo.preview.emergentagent.com/api"
